title: Notifying pig movements on eAML2
subtitle: Completing the electronic movement licence before pigs leave the holding.
excerpt: Every movement of pigs off a UK holding must be notified on eAML2 before the animals travel. This is a legal requirement, not a post-movement admin task. Getting the form right means understanding the sections, your CPH, the destination, and the 20-day standstill implications that follow every movement on and off the holding.
sourceNotes: Animal and Plant Health Agency, pig movement recording requirements, apha.gov.uk. DEFRA, eAML2 user guidance, gov.uk. British Pig Association, record keeping requirements for small-scale pig keepers.

glossaryTerms.definition: Electronic Animal Movement Licence 2, the UK online system for recording and authorising pig movements. All movements off a holding (to slaughter, to another keeper, to a show) must be submitted on eAML2 before the animals leave.

A 20-day period imposed on a holding after pigs leave or arrive that prevents other pigs from moving off that holding. Intended to limit disease spread. Exceptions apply for direct-to-slaughter movements.
County Parish Holding number — the unique identifier for your land. Required for keeping pigs, sheep, goats, or cattle. Apply via the Rural Payments Agency.

content.text: Every pig movement in the UK generates a legal obligation to notify on

before the animals move. This is not optional and there are no grace periods. Fines for unnotified movements are substantial. The system is straightforward once you have used it twice, but it requires your
, the destination CPH, and the abattoir or keeper's details to hand.

Register at eaml2.org.uk. You will need your CPH number (issued by the Rural Payments Agency when you registered to keep pigs) and your herd mark (the unique identifier printed on your ear tags). The registration process takes 24 to 48 hours to approve; do this before the first movement, not on the morning of slaughter day.

Log in and create a new movement. Enter: your CPH (source holding), the destination CPH (the abattoir or receiving holding), the number of animals and their ear tag or tattoo numbers, and the intended movement date. The abattoir's CPH is usually shown on their booking confirmation. Submit the form; both the source and destination holding receive an automatic notification.
Any movement of pigs onto or off your holding (including an abattoir run) triggers a

of 20 days. During this period, no other pigs can move off your holding. This means you cannot sell weaners within 20 days of pigs leaving for slaughter. Plan movements to avoid cascade standstills that lock your pigs on the holding for an extended period when multiple transactions overlap.

You are also required to maintain a paper or electronic movement register on the holding showing every pig arrival and departure. eAML2 exports printable records; keep them for at least three years. APHA inspectors may request them during a scheduled or unscheduled farm visit.

A direct movement to an abattoir does not trigger a standstill at the destination, but it still triggers the 20-day standstill on your own holding. Movements for vet treatment to an approved isolation unit may also be exempt. Check with APHA before each case.
